name: Panang Curry
intro: A much-loved classic Thai curry that cooks very quickly: Panang Curry! Unlike other soupy Thai curries, this curry is a spicy curry with a thick, rich, flavorful, and delicious sauce. This recipe will also cover how to make "semi-homemade" curry paste based on store-bought red curry paste.
method: To make the Panang Curry Paste, place the cumin seeds and coriander seeds and grind very finely using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Add roasted peanuts and grind again.
Add the red curry paste and shrimp paste into the mixture and mix.
To make the curry, add 1.5 teaspoons of fish sauce and oil to the beef and use your hands to rub it.
Reduce ¾ cup of coconut milk in a saucepan or skillet over medium heat until thick and creamy.
Add the Panang curry (Step 3) into the saucepan and reduce the flame. Cook for a few minutes, stirring constantly, until the coconut oil separates from the paste.
Add the palm sugar and torn kaffir lime leaves and cook for a minute.
Add the beef (Step 4) and toss quickly with the curry paste, separating the beef pieces as much as possible.
When the beef is about 50% cooked, add the remaining coconut milk and stir for another 30 seconds. Turn off the heat when done. If it seems too dry, add some water.
